name: Plaintain Empanadas
recipeIngredient: 1 small Poblano pepper, peeled and seeded
1/4 cup grated anejo, romano or feta cheese
1/4 cup grated manchego or feta cheese
Creme fraiche or sour cream (for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Cut a lengthwise slit in each plantain and place on a baking sheet. Bake until the flesh is thoroughly soft and oozing through the slit, about 40-50 minutes. Set aside to cool.
3. Meanwhile, make the stuffing: Finely dice the poblano chile. Combine with beans, scallions, cheeses, and pepper in a mixing bowl and stir to combine. The stuffing can be made up to a day in advance and refrigerated.
4. Make the dough: Peel, trim, and discard any tough ends from the plantains. Combine the plantains, banana, and salt and pulse until a smooth puree is formed or mixed until just blended. Be careful not to overwork the dough, or it will become too starchy. Wrap in plastic and chill for about 2 hours.
5. To assemble the empanadas, roll about 2 tablespoons of the dough lightly between your palms to form a ball. Place the ball of dough in the center of a tortilla press lined with a small plastic bag. Place another small plastic bag over the dough and press to form a 3 1/2-inch circle.
6. Place about 1 teaspoon of bean stuffing on half of the dough circle and fold over to enclose, pressing the edges to seal. Place stuffed empanadas on a platter and chill for at least 30 minutes or up to a day (stuffed empanadas can also be frozen).
7. To cook the empanadas, heat peanut oil in a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Fry 4-6 empanadas at a time, shaking the pan constantly, about 1 minute per side or until dark brown all over. If they darken too quickly, lower the flame slightly.
8. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with crema, creme fraiche, or sour cream for dipping.
